metadata.title: Strategic Military Bases and Installations
metadata.description: Curated dataset of globally significant military installations relevant to power projection, chokepoint control, and production network security (chanyuan politics framework)

properties.name: Yokosuka Naval Base
properties.strategic_role: Homeport of US 7th Fleet and CVN-76 Ronald Reagan. Keystone of US naval power projection in the Western Pacific. Controls sea lanes from Japan to the South China Sea.

properties.name: Sasebo Naval Base
properties.strategic_role: Forward-deployed amphibious force. Supports mine countermeasures and amphibious operations across the Western Pacific. Key to any Taiwan contingency.

properties.name: Kadena Air Base
properties.strategic_role: Largest US Air Force base in the Pacific. Hosts F-15s, KC-135s, E-3 AWACS. 260 miles from Taiwan — within unrefueled fighter range.

properties.name: Marine Corps Air Station Futenma
properties.strategic_role: Marine rotary-wing aviation in Okinawa. Controversial due to urban encroachment. Scheduled partial relocation to Camp Schwab/Henoko.

properties.name: Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni
properties.strategic_role: F-35B Lightning II forward deployment. Carrier air wing detachments. Covers the Korean Peninsula and East China Sea.

properties.name: Yokota Air Base
properties.strategic_role: Headquarters of US Forces Japan and 5th Air Force. C-130 airlift hub. Command and control node for Western Pacific operations.

properties.name: Naval Air Facility Atsugi
properties.strategic_role: Carrier air wing support and maritime patrol aircraft operations. ASW hub for Western Pacific.

properties.name: Camp Humphreys
properties.strategic_role: Largest US overseas military installation. HQ of US Forces Korea and 8th Army. 65 miles south of DMZ — deep enough for survivability.

properties.name: Osan Air Base
properties.strategic_role: 7th Air Force HQ. A-10s and F-16s for close air support and air superiority on the Korean Peninsula. THAAD radar operations.

properties.name: Kunsan Air Base
properties.strategic_role: F-16 wing positioned to interdict North Korean forces. Wolf Pack. Forward-deployed for peninsula defense.

properties.name: Andersen Air Force Base
properties.strategic_role: Strategic bomber hub — B-52, B-1B, B-2 rotational deployments. Key to second island chain defense. Missile defense and power projection node.

properties.name: Naval Base Guam
properties.strategic_role: Submarine base and logistics hub. SSN forward deployment. Critical for undersea warfare in the Western Pacific.

properties.name: Naval Support Facility Diego Garcia
properties.strategic_role: Strategic bomber staging, prepositioned ships, intelligence collection. Controls central Indian Ocean. Vital for Middle East and Indo-Pacific operations.

properties.name: RAAF Base Darwin (USMC Rotation)
properties.strategic_role: US Marine rotational force in northern Australia. Positions forces south of maritime Southeast Asia. Growing presence under AUKUS.

properties.name: Kwajalein Atoll / Reagan Test Site
properties.strategic_role: Missile defense testing and space surveillance. Tracks ICBMs and satellites across the Pacific. Part of the US early warning architecture.

properties.name: Joint Base Pearl Harbor-Hickam
properties.strategic_role: Headquarters of US Indo-Pacific Command. Pacific Fleet homeport. Central node connecting all US Pacific operations.

properties.name: Pohakuloa Training Area
properties.strategic_role: Largest military training area in the Pacific. Live fire ranges for forces rotating through the Indo-Pacific theater.

properties.name: Camp Navarro / EDCA Sites Philippines
properties.strategic_role: Enhanced Defense Cooperation Agreement rotational access. Positions US forces on the edge of the South China Sea and within range of Taiwan.

properties.name: Al Udeid Air Base
properties.strategic_role: Largest US air base in the Middle East. Combined Air Operations Center. Hub for all air operations across CENTCOM AOR.

properties.name: Al Dhafra Air Base
properties.strategic_role: F-35, F-22, tanker and ISR operations. Key node for Persian Gulf air superiority. RQ-4 Global Hawk surveillance.

properties.name: Naval Support Activity Bahrain
properties.strategic_role: Headquarters of US 5th Fleet and Naval Forces Central Command. Controls the Persian Gulf, Arabian Sea, and Red Sea. CTF-150/151/152 anti-piracy.

properties.name: Camp Arifjan
properties.strategic_role: Third Army forward headquarters. Major logistics hub and prepositioned equipment. Ground force staging for the Gulf region.

properties.name: Incirlik Air Base
properties.strategic_role: Strategic air base near Syria/Iraq. B61 nuclear weapons storage. Politically contentious — Turkey has restricted access during crises.

properties.name: Al Minhad Air Base
properties.strategic_role: Coalition support base. Australian, Canadian, and other allied access. Logistics hub for Gulf and Indian Ocean operations.

properties.name: Baghdad Diplomatic Support Center
properties.strategic_role: Residual US military advisory and training presence in Iraq. Counter-ISIS operations support.

properties.name: Erbil Air Base
properties.strategic_role: US Special Operations and ISR platform in Kurdistan. Counter-ISIS hub. Intelligence operations near Iran and Turkey.

properties.name: Fujairah Naval Facility
properties.strategic_role: Located outside the Strait of Hormuz on the Gulf of Oman side. Fallback if Hormuz is blocked. Oil tanker and naval logistics support.

properties.name: Ramstein Air Base
properties.strategic_role: Headquarters of US Air Forces in Europe. Air Mobility Command hub. Critical for all US/NATO airlift operations east. Drone operations relay.

properties.name: US European Command HQ Stuttgart
properties.strategic_role: Headquarters of US European Command and US Africa Command. Strategic command and control for two combatant commands.

properties.name: Grafenwöhr Training Area
properties.strategic_role: Largest US Army training ground in Europe. Armored brigade combat team rotational deployments. Live fire exercises.

properties.name: Aviano Air Base
properties.strategic_role: 31st Fighter Wing with F-16s. Power projection into the Mediterranean, Balkans, and Middle East. Southern European air defense.

properties.name: Naval Air Station Sigonella
properties.strategic_role: Hub of the spokes — Mediterranean maritime patrol, drone operations, logistics. MQ-4C Triton and P-8 Poseidon hub.

properties.name: Naval Support Activity Naples
properties.strategic_role: Headquarters of US Naval Forces Europe and US 6th Fleet. Adriatic and Eastern Mediterranean command and control.

properties.name: Naval Station Rota
properties.strategic_role: Aegis BMD destroyers homeport. Gateway between Atlantic and Mediterranean. Four DDG-51 destroyers for European missile defense.

properties.name: Naval Support Activity Souda Bay
properties.strategic_role: Deep-water port in Crete. Logistics and refueling for Eastern Mediterranean operations. Ammunition storage. Growing importance post-Ukraine.

properties.name: Naval Air Station Keflavik
properties.strategic_role: GIUK Gap surveillance. P-8 Poseidon ASW patrols. Monitors Russian submarine transits from Northern Fleet to the Atlantic.

properties.name: Spangdahlem Air Base
properties.strategic_role: 52nd Fighter Wing. F-16 operations for NATO's eastern flank. Close air support and suppression of enemy air defenses.

properties.name: Büchel Air Base (NATO Nuclear Share)
properties.strategic_role: B61 nuclear gravity bombs under NATO nuclear sharing. German Tornado aircraft certified for nuclear delivery.

properties.name: RAF Gibraltar
properties.strategic_role: Controls the Strait of Gibraltar — gateway between Atlantic and Mediterranean. NATO naval chokepoint monitoring.

properties.name: MK Air Base / Deveselu (Aegis Ashore Romania)
properties.strategic_role: Aegis Ashore ballistic missile defense site. SM-3 interceptors. Covers southeastern Europe from Iranian missile threats. Russia views as threatening.

properties.name: Redzikowo (Aegis Ashore Poland)
properties.strategic_role: Second European Aegis Ashore site. SM-3 Block IIA interceptors. Covers northern Europe. Russia strongly opposes as undermining nuclear deterrence.

properties.name: Camp Lemonnier
properties.strategic_role: Only permanent US base in Africa. Combined Joint Task Force-Horn of Africa. Counter-terrorism, anti-piracy, and drone operations. Controls Bab el-Mandeb approaches.

properties.name: Nigerien Air Base 201 Agadez
properties.strategic_role: MQ-9 Reaper drone hub for Sahel counter-terrorism. $110M construction. Status uncertain after 2023 Niger coup and US withdrawal order.

properties.name: Camp Simenti (Senegal)
properties.strategic_role: Cooperative security location. West Africa counter-terrorism logistics and training. Part of the distributed footprint model.

properties.name: The Pentagon / National Military Command Center
properties.strategic_role: Headquarters of the Department of Defense. Strategic command authority. All combatant command coordination flows through here.

properties.name: Naval Base San Diego
properties.strategic_role: Largest naval base on the US West Coast. Pacific Fleet surface combatant homeport. Aircraft carrier and amphibious ship berthing.

properties.name: Naval Station Norfolk
properties.strategic_role: World's largest naval station. Atlantic Fleet homeport. Multiple carrier strike groups. Supreme Allied Commander Atlantic.

properties.name: Fort Huachuca
properties.strategic_role: US Army Intelligence Center. Network Enterprise Technology Command. SIGINT and electronic warfare training.

properties.name: NORAD / Cheyenne Mountain Complex
properties.strategic_role: North American Aerospace Defense Command alternate command center. Hardened nuclear bunker. Missile warning and space surveillance.

properties.name: PLA Support Base Djibouti
properties.strategic_role: China's first overseas military base. Naval logistics and replenishment for anti-piracy. Real purpose: Indian Ocean power projection and BRI protection.

properties.name: Fiery Cross Reef (Yongshu Jiao)
properties.strategic_role: Artificial island with 3km runway, hangars, radar, CIWS, and anti-ship missiles. De facto aircraft carrier in the South China Sea. Part of SCS militarization triad.

properties.name: Subi Reef (Zhubi Jiao)
properties.strategic_role: Artificial island with runway, radars, and military installations. Northern anchor of China's SCS island chain. Helicopter and UAV operations.

properties.name: Mischief Reef (Meiji Jiao)
properties.strategic_role: Artificial island within Philippines EEZ. Runway, hangars, radomes. Eastern anchor of China's SCS fortification. Subject of 2016 Hague ruling.

properties.name: Yulin Naval Base (Sanya)
properties.strategic_role: Underground submarine pens for SSBNs and SSNs. South Sea Fleet headquarters. China's most strategically important naval facility — gateway to the SCS.

properties.name: Fuzhou / Longtian Air Base
properties.strategic_role: PLA Air Force base directly opposite Taiwan. J-16, J-20 deployments. Within minutes of Taiwan's ADIZ. Key to any cross-strait operation.

properties.name: Xiamen / Zhangzhou Missile Bases
properties.strategic_role: PLA Rocket Force short-range ballistic missile brigades aimed at Taiwan. DF-15, DF-16 deployments. Dense concentration across the Strait.

properties.name: Shanghai Jiangnan Shipyard / Changxing Island
properties.strategic_role: China's primary aircraft carrier construction facility. Type 003 Fujian built here. Heart of China's naval expansion program.

properties.name: Dalian Naval Shipyard
properties.strategic_role: Built Type 001A Shandong carrier. Major surface combatant and submarine construction. North Sea Fleet support.

properties.name: Qingdao Naval Base
properties.strategic_role: North Sea Fleet headquarters. Aircraft carrier homeport for Shandong. Major surface combatant berths. Controls Yellow Sea approaches.

properties.name: Zhoushan Naval Base
properties.strategic_role: East Sea Fleet forward base. Submarine and surface combatant berthing. Strategic position controlling approaches to Shanghai and the Yangtze.

properties.name: Guangzhou / Huangpu Shipyard
properties.strategic_role: Type 054A frigate and other combatant construction. South Sea Fleet logistics support. Pearl River Delta military-industrial complex.

properties.name: Ream Naval Base
properties.strategic_role: Chinese-built and partly Chinese-operated naval facility on the Gulf of Thailand. Gives PLA Navy access outside the SCS chokepoints. Strategically outflanks Vietnam.

properties.name: Woody Island (Yongxing Dao) Paracel Islands
properties.strategic_role: Administrative center of Sansha City. HQ-9 SAMs, J-11 fighters deployed. Oldest PLA presence in the SCS island chain.

properties.name: Haikou Air Base
properties.strategic_role: Southern Theater Command air operations. J-20 stealth fighter deployments. Covers the northern SCS approaches.

properties.name: Urumqi / Korla ASAT & Missile Test Facility
properties.strategic_role: Anti-satellite weapon testing. Ballistic missile defense development. Strategic weapons program in China's deep interior for survivability.

properties.name: Jiuquan Satellite Launch Center
properties.strategic_role: Primary crewed spaceflight launch facility. Dual-use — military satellite launches. Part of PLA Strategic Support Force space operations.

properties.name: Wenchang Space Launch Center
properties.strategic_role: Newest and largest Chinese launch facility. Heavy-lift launches for space station and military constellations. Coastal location for large boosters.

properties.name: Naval Facility Tartus
properties.strategic_role: Russia's only Mediterranean naval logistics point. Resupply and maintenance for Black Sea Fleet deployments. Anchors Russian power projection in the Eastern Med.

properties.name: Khmeimim Air Base (Hmeimim)
properties.strategic_role: Russia's main air base in Syria. Su-35, Su-34, Su-24 operations. Demonstrates Russian expeditionary air power. S-400 coverage of Eastern Mediterranean.

properties.name: Sevastopol Naval Base
properties.strategic_role: Headquarters of Russian Black Sea Fleet. Deeply contested since 2014 annexation. Submarine pens. Degraded by Ukrainian strikes since 2022 but symbolically central.

properties.name: Kaliningrad (Baltiysk Naval Base)
properties.strategic_role: Russian Baltic Fleet HQ. Iskander-M missiles. S-400 coverage. Anti-access/area denial bubble over Baltic states. Exclave creates permanent NATO vulnerability.

properties.name: Severomorsk Naval Base
properties.strategic_role: Headquarters of Russian Northern Fleet — Russia's most powerful fleet. SSBN bastion. Nuclear submarine operations in the Arctic and North Atlantic.

properties.name: Gadzhiyevo Submarine Base
properties.strategic_role: Primary base for Russian SSBNs (Delta IV, Borei class). Strategic nuclear deterrent patrols. Arctic bastioning of nuclear second-strike capability.

properties.name: Vladivostok / Pacific Fleet HQ
properties.strategic_role: Headquarters of Russian Pacific Fleet. Surface combatant and submarine base. Monitors Sea of Japan and projects power toward the North Pacific.

properties.name: Petropavlovsk-Kamchatsky (Rybachiy)
properties.strategic_role: Pacific Fleet SSBN base. Borei-class deployments. Direct access to open Pacific for nuclear patrols. One of Russia's most survivable nuclear assets.

properties.name: Cam Ranh Bay (Russian access agreement)
properties.strategic_role: Logistics and resupply arrangement (not full base). Gives Russian Navy access to South China Sea approaches and the Indian Ocean. Historic Soviet-era facility.

properties.name: Kubinka Air Base / Patriot Park Complex
properties.strategic_role: Russian Aerospace Forces demonstration and test facility. S-400 and advanced weapons showcase. Strategic communications function.

properties.name: Sarov (Arzamas-16) Nuclear Weapons Complex
properties.strategic_role: Primary Russian nuclear weapons design laboratory (VNIIEF). Equivalent of Los Alamos. Nuclear warhead design, testing, and production.

properties.name: Omsk / Central Military District HQ
properties.strategic_role: Central Military District command and control. Reserve mobilization hub. Strategic depth in Russia's industrial Urals-Siberian corridor.

properties.name: Plesetsk Cosmodrome
properties.strategic_role: Primary Russian military space launch facility. ICBM testing (RS-28 Sarmat). Satellite launches for GLONASS and military constellations.

properties.name: 201st Military Base Tajikistan
properties.strategic_role: Russia's largest overseas ground force deployment. Secures Central Asian buffer zone. Monitors Afghan border. Controls Tajik political stability.

properties.name: Kant Air Base Kyrgyzstan
properties.strategic_role: Russian air base under CSTO framework. Su-25 attack aircraft. Central Asian force projection. Near former US facility at Manas.

properties.name: 102nd Military Base Gyumri Armenia
properties.strategic_role: Russian ground forces in the South Caucasus. Presence near Turkish border. Post-2020 Nagorno-Karabakh role increasingly questioned.

properties.name: Nagurskoye Air Base (Franz Josef Land)
properties.strategic_role: Russia's northernmost military base. Arctic Trefoil facility. Controls Northern Sea Route. MiG-31 interceptors. S-300 air defense. Arctic resource competition.

properties.name: Novaya Zemlya Nuclear Test Site
properties.strategic_role: Historic nuclear weapons testing ground. Tsar Bomba detonated here 1961. Maintained capability for resumed testing. Nuclear warhead storage and monitoring.

properties.name: Mozdok Air Base
properties.strategic_role: Strategic bomber base in North Caucasus. Tu-22M3 Backfire operations. Forward base for South Caucasus and Middle East power projection.

properties.name: HMNB Portsmouth
properties.strategic_role: Homeport of Queen Elizabeth-class aircraft carriers. Royal Navy headquarters. UK's primary surface fleet base.

properties.name: HMNB Clyde (Faslane)
properties.strategic_role: UK's nuclear deterrent — Vanguard-class SSBNs with Trident II D5 missiles. Astute-class SSN base. Continuous At Sea Deterrent (CASD).

properties.name: GCHQ Bude (ECHELON)
properties.strategic_role: Transatlantic cable intercept station. Part of Five Eyes ECHELON network. Taps undersea fiber optic cables for SIGINT collection.

properties.name: HMS Juffair (UK Naval Support Facility Bahrain)
properties.strategic_role: East of Suez naval presence restored. UK mine countermeasures and patrol vessels. Persian Gulf maritime security operations.

properties.name: Gibraltar Naval Base
properties.strategic_role: Royal Navy presence at the Strait of Gibraltar. Nuclear submarine port of call. Monitors maritime traffic between Atlantic and Mediterranean.

properties.name: RAF Ascension Island
properties.strategic_role: Mid-Atlantic staging point. Proved vital in 1982 Falklands War. Supports South Atlantic patrol and signals intelligence.

properties.name: RAF Akrotiri / Dhekelia (Cyprus)
properties.strategic_role: Sovereign Base Areas in Cyprus. Eastern Mediterranean air operations. Intelligence collection. Staging for Middle East operations.

properties.name: Toulon Naval Base
properties.strategic_role: Homeport of French aircraft carrier Charles de Gaulle. Mediterranean Fleet headquarters. France's primary naval power projection platform.

properties.name: Brest Naval Base (Île Longue)
properties.strategic_role: French SSBN base — Triomphant-class with M51 SLBMs. France's independent nuclear deterrent. Force océanique stratégique.

properties.name: French Forces Djibouti (FFDj)
properties.strategic_role: Largest French overseas base. Foreign Legion, naval, air elements. Controls Bab el-Mandeb and supports operations across the Sahel and Indian Ocean.

properties.name: French Forces Southern Indian Ocean (La Réunion)
properties.strategic_role: French naval and air patrol hub for southern Indian Ocean. EEZ surveillance of France's vast maritime territories. Intelligence collection.

properties.name: French Naval Base Abu Dhabi
properties.strategic_role: France's first permanent military base in the Gulf. Naval logistics and Rafale fighter deployments. Balances US dominance with independent French presence.

properties.name: French Forces New Caledonia
properties.strategic_role: French military presence in the Southwest Pacific. Maritime surveillance. Strategic nickel reserves protection. Balances China's Pacific island diplomacy.

properties.name: NATO Cooperative Cyber Defence Centre of Excellence (Tallinn)
properties.strategic_role: NATO's primary cyber warfare research center. Tallinn Manual legal framework. Frontline in Baltic cyber defense against Russian operations.

properties.name: NATO Enhanced Forward Presence Latvia
properties.strategic_role: Multinational battlegroup led by Canada. Tripwire force on Russia's border. Part of post-2014 NATO eastern reinforcement.

properties.name: NATO Enhanced Forward Presence Lithuania
properties.strategic_role: German-led multinational battlegroup. Suwalki Gap defense — the 65-mile corridor between Kaliningrad and Belarus that could sever the Baltic states.

properties.name: NATO Enhanced Forward Presence Poland
properties.strategic_role: US-led multinational battlegroup. Brigade-level presence expanding. Poland as NATO's eastern anchor and primary conventional deterrent.

properties.name: NATO Forward Presence Romania (Cincu)
properties.strategic_role: French-led multinational battlegroup. Black Sea security. Reinforced after Russia's 2022 invasion of Ukraine. Monitors Crimea and the western Black Sea.

properties.name: NATO Allied Air Command (Ramstein)
properties.strategic_role: NATO integrated air and missile defense command. Controls the NATO Air Policing missions across the alliance. Coordinates air defense from Iceland to Turkey.

properties.name: NATO Allied Joint Force Command Naples
properties.strategic_role: NATO's southern region command. Oversees Mediterranean operations. Commands NATO Response Force and coordinates with EUNAVFOR.

properties.name: NATO Allied Joint Force Command Brunssum
properties.strategic_role: NATO's northern region command. Oversees operations from Arctic to northeastern flank. Coordinates Baltic and Nordic defense.

properties.name: Berga Naval Base (Sweden NATO accession)
properties.strategic_role: Swedish Navy headquarters. Post-2024 NATO accession transforms Baltic security. Advanced submarine capability. Controls central Baltic approaches.

properties.name: Ørland Air Station (Norway)
properties.strategic_role: Norwegian F-35A main operating base. NATO Quick Reaction Alert for High North. Monitors Russian Northern Fleet sorties. Arctic air superiority.

properties.name: Tikkakoski Air Base (Finland NATO accession)
properties.strategic_role: Finnish Air Force HQ. F-35 transition underway. Post-2023 NATO accession adds 1,340km border with Russia. Transforms northern European defense geometry.

properties.name: Turkish Naval Forces Command (Istanbul)
properties.strategic_role: Controls the Turkish Straits — Bosporus and Dardanelles. Montreux Convention enforcement. Gatekeeper between Black Sea and Mediterranean.

properties.name: Konya Air Base
properties.strategic_role: Turkish Air Force F-16 operations and AWACS. NATO training center. Central Anatolian strategic depth.

properties.name: Turkish Military Base Mogadishu (TURKSOM)
properties.strategic_role: Turkey's largest overseas military base. Trains Somali forces. Projects Turkish influence into the Horn of Africa and Indian Ocean approaches.

properties.name: Turkish Military Base Qatar
properties.strategic_role: Turkish deployment in the Gulf. Supported Qatar during 2017 blockade. Demonstrates Turkey's post-Ottoman Gulf ambitions. ~3,000 troops.

properties.name: Akinci Air Base
properties.strategic_role: Site of 2016 coup attempt. Turkish Air Force headquarters. Strategic bomber and tanker operations. Central command and control.

properties.name: INS Baaz (Campbell Bay) Andaman & Nicobar
properties.strategic_role: India's southernmost naval air station. 90 miles from Strait of Malacca entrance. Dominates the chokepoint between Indian and Pacific Oceans.

properties.name: INS Kardip (Kamorta) Andaman & Nicobar
properties.strategic_role: Naval forward operating base in the Andamans. Extends Indian maritime domain awareness across the Bay of Bengal and Andaman Sea.

properties.name: INS Shivaji / Karwar Naval Base (Project Seabird)
properties.strategic_role: India's largest naval base on the western coast. Aircraft carrier berthing. Expanding to become India's primary blue-water naval hub. Arabian Sea control.

properties.name: INS Adyar / Eastern Naval Command (Visakhapatnam)
properties.strategic_role: Eastern Naval Command HQ. Submarine base. Controls Bay of Bengal. Nuclear submarine (INS Arihant class) operations and deterrent patrols.

properties.name: INS Angre / Western Naval Command (Mumbai)
properties.strategic_role: Western Naval Command HQ. Arabian Sea and Persian Gulf operations. Protects India's western trade routes and energy imports through Hormuz.

properties.name: Kure Naval Base (JMSDF)
properties.strategic_role: Japan Maritime Self-Defense Force base. Izumo-class helicopter destroyer homeport. Seto Inland Sea industrial area protection.

properties.name: Sasebo JMSDF Base
properties.strategic_role: JMSDF fleet base alongside US presence. Osumi-class landing ships. Southwestern island defense and amphibious operations.

properties.name: JSDF Yonaguni Garrison
properties.strategic_role: Japan's westernmost military installation. Electronic warfare and coastal monitoring unit. Only 111km from Taiwan. Critical intelligence outpost.

properties.name: JSDF Miyako Island Garrison
properties.strategic_role: Anti-ship and SAM missile batteries on the Miyako Strait. Controls the key passage between Okinawa and Taiwan where Chinese Navy must transit.

properties.name: Naha Air Base (JASDF)
properties.strategic_role: JASDF F-15J wing. QRA intercepts of Chinese aircraft near Senkaku Islands. Japan's first-response southwestern air defense.

properties.name: Chitose Air Base (JASDF)
properties.strategic_role: Northern defense against Russian air incursions. F-15J interceptors. Monitors Russian Pacific Fleet air activity near Hokkaido.

properties.name: Pine Gap (Joint Defence Facility)
properties.strategic_role: CIA/NSA ground station. Satellite intelligence collection and missile early warning. One of the most important Five Eyes intelligence facilities globally.

properties.name: RAAF Base Tindal
properties.strategic_role: Northern Australia F-35A operations. US bomber rotational deployments. Runway being extended for B-52 operations under AUKUS force posture initiatives.

properties.name: Woomera Test Range
properties.strategic_role: Weapons testing and space launch facility. Hypersonic vehicle testing. Largest land-based test range in the Western world.

properties.name: HMAS Stirling (Fleet Base West)
properties.strategic_role: Indian Ocean naval base. Future AUKUS nuclear submarine base. Collins-class submarine homeport. Controls southwestern Indian Ocean approaches.

properties.name: Naval Communication Station Harold E. Holt (Exmouth)
properties.strategic_role: VLF submarine communication station. Transmits launch orders to US and allied submarines in the Indian Ocean and Western Pacific.

properties.name: Nevatim Air Base
properties.strategic_role: Israeli Air Force F-35I Adir main base. Long-range strike capability. Potential staging for Iran strike operations.

properties.name: Palmachim Air Base
properties.strategic_role: Jericho ballistic missile launches. Space launch vehicle facility. Arrow missile defense system. Israel's primary strategic deterrent site.

properties.name: Dimona Nuclear Research Center
properties.strategic_role: Israel's nuclear weapons production facility (undeclared). Estimated 80-400 warheads. Plutonium reactor and reprocessing. Policy of nuclear ambiguity.

properties.name: Unit 8200 Headquarters (Herzliya/Glilot)
properties.strategic_role: Israel's premier SIGINT unit — equivalent of NSA. Cyber warfare, signals intelligence. Feeds the intelligence-industrial complex. Pegasus spyware origins.

properties.name: Prince Sultan Air Base
properties.strategic_role: Major Saudi air base south of Riyadh. US deployed Patriot batteries and F-15Es here. Combined Air Operations Center for Gulf operations.

properties.name: King Faisal Naval Base (Jeddah)
properties.strategic_role: Saudi Western Fleet on the Red Sea. Controls approaches to Bab el-Mandeb from the north. Houthi drone and missile defense operations.

properties.name: Tehran / IRGC Aerospace Force HQ
properties.strategic_role: Headquarters of IRGC Aerospace Force. Ballistic missile and drone program coordination. Strategic deterrence command for the Persian Gulf region.

properties.name: Bandar Abbas Naval Base
properties.strategic_role: IRIN and IRGCN headquarters for the Strait of Hormuz. Fast attack craft, midget submarines, mine warfare. Iran's primary chokepoint control asset.

properties.name: Jask Naval Base
properties.strategic_role: New Iranian naval base east of the Strait of Hormuz. Gives Iran naval presence outside the chokepoint in the Gulf of Oman. Strategic diversification.

properties.name: Abu Musa Island Garrison
properties.strategic_role: Iranian military garrison on disputed island in the central Persian Gulf. Controls shipping lanes. Anti-ship missiles and artillery positioned against tanker traffic.

properties.name: Isfahan Nuclear Technology Center
properties.strategic_role: Uranium conversion facility. Part of Iran's nuclear fuel cycle. IAEA-monitored but subject to enrichment escalation. Underground hardened facilities nearby at Natanz.

properties.name: Shahrud Missile Test Facility
properties.strategic_role: IRGC ballistic missile and space launch vehicle testing. Medium and intermediate-range missile development. Satellite launches with dual-use technology.

properties.name: Mayang-do Submarine Base
properties.strategic_role: North Korea's primary submarine base. Sinpo-class SLBM submarine development. Potential nuclear second-strike capability under development.

properties.name: Yongbyon Nuclear Scientific Research Center
properties.strategic_role: North Korea's primary nuclear weapons material production site. 5MWe reactor, reprocessing plant, centrifuge enrichment. Source of plutonium and HEU for warheads.

properties.name: Tonghae / Musudan-ri Satellite Launch Station
properties.strategic_role: ICBM and space launch vehicle testing facility. Hwasong series launches. Demonstrates North Korean ability to target CONUS and global reach ambitions.

properties.name: US Naval Air Station Bermuda (Former)
properties.strategic_role: Historic mid-Atlantic ASW base. Closed 1995 but reactivation potential. Covers Atlantic sea lanes and hurricane response staging.

properties.name: Naval Station Guantanamo Bay
properties.strategic_role: Oldest US overseas naval base. Controls Windward Passage between Cuba and Haiti. Strategic value as Caribbean chokepoint and detention facility.

properties.name: Basa Air Base (EDCA site)
properties.strategic_role: Philippine Air Force main jet fighter base. F-16 acquisition planned. EDCA site for US rotational access. Controls Luzon Strait approaches.

properties.name: Antonio Bautista Air Base (Palawan)
properties.strategic_role: Westernmost Philippine air base. Directly overlooks the South China Sea and Spratly Islands. Key for Philippine EEZ surveillance.

properties.name: Changi Naval Base
properties.strategic_role: Singapore's primary naval facility. US Navy logistics hub through cooperation agreement. Controls eastern entrance to the Strait of Malacca.

properties.name: Paya Lebar Air Base / Tengah
properties.strategic_role: RSAF F-35B acquisition underway. US military logistics access. Air defense of the Malacca Strait approaches. Disproportionate capability for small state.

properties.name: ROK Joint Chiefs of Staff / Yongsan-Pyeongtaek
properties.strategic_role: South Korean military command center. Wartime operational control transition from US. Directs 600,000 active-duty forces.

properties.name: Jinhae Naval Base
properties.strategic_role: ROK Navy headquarters and largest naval base. Aegis destroyer homeport. Controls Korea Strait and Sea of Japan approaches.

properties.name: Ching Chuan Kang Air Base (CCK)
properties.strategic_role: Major ROCAF base in central Taiwan. F-16V and IDF fighters. Underground hardened shelters. Key to air defense of the western coast against PLA air attacks.

properties.name: Zuoying Naval Base (Kaohsiung)
properties.strategic_role: ROC Navy main fleet base. Frigate and corvette homeport. Indigenous submarine construction program. Bashi Channel and South China Sea surveillance.

properties.name: Hualien Air Base (Jiashan Mountain)
properties.strategic_role: Mountain-carved hardened air base on Taiwan's east coast. Survivable against first strike. F-16V squadrons. Protected by the Central Mountain Range.

properties.name: Pingtung Air Base
properties.strategic_role: Southern Taiwan air defense. P-3C maritime patrol aircraft. Controls Bashi Channel — critical passage between Philippine Sea and South China Sea.

properties.name: Ho Chi Minh City / Tan Son Nhat Military Section
properties.strategic_role: Vietnamese Air Force Southern Command. Su-30MK2 operations. Covers southern approaches to the South China Sea.

properties.name: Cam Ranh International Port / Cam Ranh Bay
properties.strategic_role: Vietnam's most strategically located naval base. Kilo-class submarine homeport. Overlooks South China Sea. Historic Soviet, now Vietnamese with selective access.

properties.name: Egyptian Naval Base Berenice
properties.strategic_role: Egypt's new southern Red Sea naval base. Extends Egyptian maritime control toward Bab el-Mandeb. Built with UAE support. Red Sea security amid Houthi threat.

properties.name: Suez Canal Zone Military Installations
properties.strategic_role: Egyptian military installations along the Suez Canal. Controls the most important maritime chokepoint connecting Mediterranean and Indian Ocean. 12% of global trade.

properties.name: Port of Massawa (Eritrea)
properties.strategic_role: Red Sea port with historical Italian colonial naval heritage. UAE and others have sought access. Controls central Red Sea approaches near Yemen conflict zone.

properties.name: UAE Base Assab (Eritrea)
properties.strategic_role: UAE military logistics base on the Red Sea. Supported Yemen intervention. Projects Emirati power across Bab el-Mandeb and the Horn of Africa.

properties.name: U-Tapao Royal Thai Navy Airfield
properties.strategic_role: Thai naval air base with US exercise access. Cobra Gold exercise staging. Strategic location between Gulf of Thailand and Andaman Sea near the Kra Isthmus.

properties.name: US Army Garrison Yongsan (Legacy/Transition)
properties.strategic_role: Historic USFK headquarters in Seoul. Transitioning to Camp Humphreys. Symbolic US commitment to Korean Peninsula security since 1953.

properties.name: PLA Navy Potential Facility (Solomon Islands / Pacific)
properties.strategic_role: China's expanding security agreements with Pacific Island states (Solomons, Kiribati). No confirmed base yet but port access agreements signal power projection ambitions.

properties.name: Indian Naval Listening Station (Madagascar - Assumption Island negotiations)
properties.strategic_role: India negotiating Indian Ocean island access for naval intelligence and maritime surveillance. Part of Indo-Pacific competition with China's String of Pearls.

properties.name: Fernando de Noronha Tracking Station (Brazil)
properties.strategic_role: Brazilian military tracking station in the mid-Atlantic. South Atlantic chokepoint monitoring. Part of Brazil's Blue Amazon maritime defense concept.

properties.name: Pakistan Naval Dockyard Karachi
properties.strategic_role: Pakistan Navy headquarters and main base. Arabian Sea operations. Receives Chinese naval technology transfers. Balances Indian naval dominance.

properties.name: Gwadar Port (Dual-use)
properties.strategic_role: China-Pakistan Economic Corridor terminus. Commercial port with potential PLA Navy access. 400km from Strait of Hormuz. Strategic pearl in China's Indian Ocean strategy.

properties.name: Hambantota Port (Sri Lanka)
properties.strategic_role: Chinese-leased port on 99-year agreement after Sri Lankan debt default. Strategic Indian Ocean position on major shipping lanes. Debt-trap diplomacy case study.

properties.name: Indian Radar Station (Maldives - historical)
properties.strategic_role: Indian military radar presence in the Maldives. Monitors central Indian Ocean. Chinese influence has complicated India-Maldives defense ties since 2018.

properties.name: Chinese Logistics Facility (Eritrea - reported)
properties.strategic_role: Reported Chinese dual-use logistics facility near the Red Sea. Part of expanding PLA Navy logistics network. Controls western Indian Ocean approaches.

properties.name: Incirlik / Kurecik Radar Station
properties.strategic_role: AN/TPY-2 forward-based radar for NATO missile defense. Detects Iranian ballistic missiles. Data feeds to Aegis Ashore sites in Romania and Poland.

properties.name: THAAD Battery Seongju
properties.strategic_role: Terminal High Altitude Area Defense system. Controversial due to Chinese opposition (radar range covers Chinese territory). North Korean missile defense.

properties.name: JASDF Hamamatsu / AWACS Base
properties.strategic_role: E-767 AWACS and E-2D Advanced Hawkeye operations. Airborne early warning for Japanese airspace. Command and control of air defense.

properties.name: JUSMAG Thailand (Cobra Gold)
properties.strategic_role: Joint US Military Advisory Group. Cobra Gold — largest multilateral exercise in the Indo-Pacific. Maintains US-Thailand alliance interoperability.

properties.name: Chinese Military Cooperation (Tajikistan - reported)
properties.strategic_role: Reported PLA outpost in eastern Tajikistan's Wakhan Corridor area. Monitors Afghanistan border and Central Asian stability. Chinese security perimeter expansion.

properties.name: Baikonur Cosmodrome (Russian lease)
properties.strategic_role: World's first and largest operational space launch facility. Russian crewed space launches. Military satellite deployments. Leased from Kazakhstan.

properties.name: Mers-el-Kebir Naval Base (Algeria)
properties.strategic_role: Major Algerian naval base with Kilo-class submarines. Western Mediterranean maritime presence. Russian-equipped navy with Mediterranean reach.

properties.name: JSDF Facility Djibouti
properties.strategic_role: Japan's only overseas military facility. Anti-piracy operations. First Japanese foreign military presence since WWII. Signals normalized Japanese security role.

properties.name: Italian Military Support Base Djibouti
properties.strategic_role: Italian military logistics in Djibouti. Supports EU anti-piracy operations (EUNAVFOR). Part of Djibouti's unique concentration of foreign military bases.

properties.name: NATO Joint Analysis and Lessons Learned Centre (Portugal)
properties.strategic_role: Strategic analysis center. While not a combat base, represents NATO's Atlantic dimension. Azores/Lajes Field nearby historically critical for Atlantic ASW.

properties.name: SAS Algoa (Port Elizabeth) / Simon's Town Naval Base
properties.strategic_role: South African Navy base at the Cape. Controls the Cape of Good Hope route — alternative to Suez Canal. Strategic for tanker traffic during Suez disruptions.

properties.name: RAF Mount Pleasant (Falkland Islands)
properties.strategic_role: UK military garrison in the Falklands. Typhoon fighters and Rapier SAM. Controls Drake Passage and South Atlantic maritime approaches. Reassurance against Argentine claims.
